mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Top-quartile PAYEMS growth months do not produce significantly higher average 20-day SPX returns than bottom-quartile months over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rising above 5.5 percent indicates labor market deterioration that precedes corporate earnings downgrades and consumer retrenchment, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5% are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in real retail sales and industrial production generate a joint demand-supply expansion signal that amplifies earnings expectations beyond either series alone, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and INDPRO does not significantly predict 20-day SPX returns (p > 0.05) in a rolling 3-year multivariate regression, or the combined signal underperforms the best single-series signal.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to hike faster than discounted by markets, raising real discount rates and compressing equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During identified tightening cycles, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases fail to produce negative average 20-day SPX returns (mean return >= 0 or t-test p > 0.05), or the effect size is indistinguishable from easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts signals weakening construction activity and durable goods demand, which reduces expected aggregate output and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month changes in HOUST do not coincide with negative average 5-day SPX returns (mean return >= 0 or t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery indicates rising capacity utilization and supply-side repair, which supports corporate margin expansion and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO increases fail to predict positive average 60-day SPX returns (mean return <= 0 or t-test p > 0.05) during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2 percent raises short-term borrowing costs, tightens financial conditions, and increases the equity risk premium, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days while the threshold remains exceeded.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS above 2.0% do not produce significantly negative average 5-day SPX returns compared to periods below 2.0% over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is below 5 percent, rising consumer prices signal an overheating labor market that forces unexpected Federal Reserve tightening, and the interaction of low UNRATE and accelerating CPIAUCSL predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE and CPIAUCSL is not significant (p > 0.05) in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns when UNRATE is below 5.0%, or the signal fails to outperform a single-series CPIAUCSL model.
